Cleans the bridge daemon source-comment layer by replacing ticket/date archaeology with durable descriptions around wake-event delivery, singleton/log persistence, broadcast wake routing, harness focus defaults, Codex fail-closed focus handling, and cleanup behavior. Runtime behavior and operator-visible warning strings are unchanged.

Evidence: L1 (comment-only static source diff, focused diagnostic, syntax check, diff whitespace checks) → L1 required (comment cleanup ACs; no runtime behavior ACs in this batch). Residual: remaining grouped #11925 surfaces outside `ai/daemons/bridge/daemon.mjs`.

## Deltas from ticket
This is a focused third #11925 batch after merged PRs #11947 and #11971. It deliberately leaves the two historical issue-number references embedded in runtime warning strings, because preserving operator-visible diagnostics is safer than silently changing them in a comment cleanup PR.

### 🔬 Depth Floor

**Documented search (per guide §7.1):**

I actively looked for (1) load-bearing operator-context loss in the Codex fail-closed guard rewrite (the stripped block referenced `@neo-gpt`'s `harnessTarget: 'disabled'` as defense-in-depth rationale — the new prose preserves the conditional-guard intent without the operator-name coupling, so the future "accidental re-enable" case is still naturally caught by the `if (appName === 'Codex' && !focusSeedKey)` conditional), (2) runtime-string drift (`rg "#[0-9]{4,}"` on diff = 0 deltas in template-literal-quoted strings; both remaining matches are intentionally preserved warning strings per PR body), and (3) silent stripping of load-bearing single-key-vs-multi-step `focusSeedKey`/`focusSeedSequence` distinction (the new comment retains the explicit "must use an explicit `focusSeedSequence` implementation rather than masquerading as a single-key `focusSeedKey` opt-in" prose — the substrate primitive distinction survives the cleanup, which was my biggest concern given how easily it could have collapsed into "use focusSeedKey").

One non-blocking observation: the new Codex guard prose ends with *"The app-server adapter may eventually supersede the UI-keystroke path entirely."* This is mildly speculative durable-intent — it's a reasonable signpost for future maintainers but flirts with rhetorical-drift territory. Not a Required Action because (a) the adapter primitive (#10517) is a known and tracked roadmap item, not invented in this PR, and (b) "may eventually" is appropriately hedged. Worth flagging as a watch-pattern for future comment cleanups: speculative forward-looking-statements in source comments can quietly drift from the actual roadmap state.

**Rhetorical-Drift Audit (per guide §7.4):**

- [x] PR description framing matches diff: "replaces ticket/date archaeology with durable descriptions" — verified
- [x] No Anchor & Echo JSDoc additions to audit (cleanup is reductive)
- [x] No `[RETROSPECTIVE]` tag in PR body to audit
- [x] Linked anchors: PR body cites #11947 + #11971 as precedent merges — both verified merged and substrate-comparable

**Findings:** Pass
